summary: Jeff Atwood addresses the growing problem of comment spam on his blog, which includes typical spam topics like viagra and gambling. He values open discussion through comments but recognizes spam as a serious threat to that openness. He recommends MT-blacklist, an anti-spam plugin for Movable Type, which he found easy to install and effective. The post ends with a defiant message to spammers.
key_points: Comment spam (viagra, gambling, etc.) was becoming a major problem for blogs in 2004
Open commenting is valuable for two-way discussion but requires spam protection
MT-blacklist provided an effective, easy-to-install solution for Movable Type users
money_quotes: I like the way anyone can leave a comment regarding my posts – the only good discussion is a two-way discussion – but the comment spam is getting way out of hand
key_insight: Maintaining open discussion on blogs requires active measures to combat spam, but good tools can make that fight manageable.
